items.title: Pau Cubarsí: “Hemos callado muchas bocas”
items.summary: El defensor de la Selección España aseguró que el equipo respondió a las críticas con actuaciones en la cancha. Además, destacó la fortaleza defensiva de La Roja, que solo ha recibido un gol en la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026.

items.title: March for democracy planned for anniversary of historic MLK-led 1963 Washington march
items.summary: A large coalition led by the Rev. Al Sharpton and the family of the Rev. Martin Luther King Jr. will mark the anniversary of the historic March on Washington next month with a march for democracy

items.title: 74-year-old Florida man becomes oldest inmate executed in the state’s modern history
items.summary: 74-year-old Florida man becomes oldest inmate executed in the state’s modern history

items.title: Dallas se llena de vida con las aficiones de Francia y España, jugando por un boleto a la final
items.summary: El ambiente no pudo ser mejor. Francia y España chocaron en las semifinales del Mundial y las gradas tuvieron a los mejores fanáticos.

items.title: Oyarzabal habla de la importancia del mediocampo para crear presión a la portería francesa
items.summary: El autor del primer gol de la semifinal, Mikel Oyarzabal afirma que el equipo español está tranquilo con lo que han conseguido en el torneo. Confía y le da protagonismo al trabajo de mediocampo, que les ha dado el segundo tanto.

items.title: Pepe del Bosque: “Argentina necesita la mejor versión de Messi” | Conexión Mundial
items.summary: La Selección de Argentina se enfrenta al rival más fuerte en esta Copa Mundial y el delantero será una parte fundamental en la búsqueda del pase a la gran final.

items.title: Rayan Charki: "Nos vimos superados, la decepción es enorme"
items.summary: El jugador galo, que entró en el segundo tiempo en lugar de Michael Olisé, lamentó que aunque el juego ante España por la semifinal era 'el partido de la vida', la selección se vio superada y no pudieron conseguir el pase al partido definitorio del título

items.title: ¡A la Final del Mundial! Yamal y España festejaron a ritmo de Rosalía y Aitana tras vencer a Francia
items.summary: En la cancha y ante su afición, la Roja explotó a ritmo de Rosalía y Aitana, Luis de la Fuente no pudo contener la emoción y los jugadores se desbordaron en una celebración histórica. Revive todo lo que no se vio del primer finalista del Mundial.

items.title: España ya está en la final y revive el sueño de 2010 | Todo el Mundial
items.summary: Andrés Guardado, Miguel Gurwitz y José María Gutiérrez "Guti" analizaron la contundente victoria de España por 2-0 sobre Francia para clasificar a la final de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026. Además, repasaron el partido por el título.

items.title: España acaba con el sueño de Mbappé y Francia: está en la final del Mundial 2026.
items.summary: El conjunto de Luis de la Fuente ofreció una exhibición de principio a fin para clasificarse a la gran final de la Copa del Mundo, donde intentará conquistar su segundo campeonato.

items.title: Deschamps lamenta irse del banquillo francés sin llegar a la final: "Fuimos inferiores"
items.summary: El entrenador de la selección francesa, Didier Deschamps, mostró la gran desilusión después de caer en la semifinal del Mundial ante España y verse superados por un equipo que controló el juego y lo dejó con la opción solo del tercer lugar.

items.title: Maine Democrats criticize GOP Sen. Susan Collins after the fatal ICE shooting in the state
items.summary: Maine Democrats are fighting to link Republican Sen. Susan Collins to a new fatal shooting by Immigration and Customs Enforcement in their state

items.title: Senate Democrats block $1 trillion defense bill in protest over Iran war
items.summary: Senate Democrats have blocked a $1 trillion annual defense bill in protest of President Donald Trump’s war against Iran

items.title: Tropical depression forms far offshore of Mexico and is forecast to strengthen
items.summary: Forecasters say a tropical depression has formed far offshore of Mexico and could strengthen into a hurricane this week

items.title: De la Fuente: “Enfrente tenían al mejor equipo del mundo”
items.summary: Feliz de dirigir a España a su segunda final del Mundial, Luis De la Fuente afirma que estar ahí es un lujo. El ‘míster’ asegura que, aunque enfrentaron a una de las selecciones más grandes, Francia se midió ante el mejor equipo del mundo.

items.title: House passes Trump-backed bill that would make daylight saving time permanent
items.summary: The legislation, known as the Sunshine Protection Act and backed by President Donald Trump, now heads to the Senate for consideration.

items.title: Man fleeing immigration officers in Florida is struck and killed by tractor trailer, police say
items.summary: Authorities say a man running from an encounter with immigration and other federal agents in Florida was struck and killed by a tractor trailer

items.title: Spain sparkles to shock France 2-0 and advance to the World Cup final
items.summary: Spain is back in the men's World Cup final for the first time since 2010. That's also when Spain won its only World Cup title. France had hoped to reach the final for the third straight tournament.

items.title: Pedro Porro se escapa y define como crack para el 0-2 de España sobre Francia
items.summary: El jugador del Tottenham sorprendió con su llegada al área y definió con una exquisitez absoluta. El rostro de Kylian Mbappé lo decía todo: devastación total.

items.title: How do young people feel about AI? 7 teens weigh in
items.summary: What's it like to grow up and learn in the age of AI? NPR put that question to seven teenagers across the country.

items.title: In the aftermath of deadly shootings, ICE pauses most traffic stops
items.summary: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement will pause non-urgent vehicle stops after two deadly shootings in less than a week, Maine U.S. Sen. Angus King's office tells NPR.

items.title: The best portable fans to keep you cool, heatwave tested and reviewed by editors
items.summary: We tested the best personal fans, including handheld, desk, and neck fans, from Shark, Jisulife and more.

items.title: Darline Graham is sworn in as senator to fill brother Lindsey Graham’s seat
items.summary: Darline Graham, previously not a politician or a public figure, is expected to be a reliable vote for the Republican majority.

items.title: Man gets prison in deadly shooting of ex-girlfriend as she pushed baby's stroller
items.summary: A New York man who was convicted of killing his ex-girlfriend as she pushed their baby in a stroller has been sentenced to 25 years to life in prison

items.title: 'I'm Joe Lombardo': Nevada governor pulled over in traffic stop
items.summary: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo was pulled over in a May traffic stop in Las Vegas and wasn't ticketed

items.title: E. Jean Carroll receives $5.6 million from Trump in sexual abuse and defamation case
items.summary: The payment comes three years after a jury found President Trump liable for sexually abusing and defaming the writer.

items.title: Josh Grisetti, ‘The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel’ and Broadway actor, dies at 44
items.summary: Josh Grisetti, who appeared in the Broadway musical “Something Rotten” and Amazon Prime Video’s “The Marvelous Mrs.

items.title: What it means when federal authorities say cars are being weaponized
items.summary: The term weaponized vehicle has become commonplace at news conferences and in statements released by federal officials during the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown

items.title: ICE issues temporary nationwide order for officers to stop vehicle pursuits
items.summary: ICE has issued a temporary nationwide order to its officers to stop pursuing people in vehicles. NBC News' Julia Ainsley has details on the order that comes after another ICE-involved shooting in Maine.

items.title: Sully Sullenberger, ‘Miracle on the Hudson’ pilot, says he has Alzheimer’s disease
items.summary: Chesley “Sully” Sullenberger announced Tuesday that he has been diagnosed with Alzheimer’s disease.

items.title: Family says US seismologist has been detained in China for nearly 2 years with no trial
items.summary: A China-born U.S. seismologist has been detained in China without trial for nearly two years

items.title: Environmental groups sue government to stop a big change to the Endangered Species Act
items.summary: By altering the definition of the word "harm" as used by the Endangered Species Act, the Trump administration may limit how wildlife is protected in the United States. Environmental groups are suing.

items.title: Outdoor rugs are on major sale at Bed Bath & Beyond, with items up to 70% off
items.summary: Bed Bath & Beyond is having a major sale on outdoor rugs, with popular picks up to 70% off and available in many shapes and sizes.

items.title: Gold trader gets time served in Iran sanctions case that strained US-Turkey relations
items.summary: A gold trader who admitted conspiring with a Turkish bank to help Iran evade U.S. sanctions is set to be sentenced to time served

items.title: Don’t let the spikes scare you — an acupressure mat could be the recovery tool you need
items.summary: Acupressure mats help relieve muscle tension and stress, plus can lead to better sleep. Experts share how to pick one, and why they’re not called acupuncture mats.

items.title: Investigative journalist reports on the abuse inside ICE's largest detention facility
items.summary: The New Yorker writer Jonathan Blitzer says thousands of people are being held in tents in the El Paso desert, where inhumane conditions have become a tool to pressure people to accept deportation.

items.title: Some international artists are skipping U.S. tours. Others may follow suit
items.summary: The visa process for visiting artists has always been complicated and expensive. Under the current administration, it's gotten significantly worse.

items.title: Inflation slowed sharply -- but it may not last
items.summary: Annual inflation hit 3.5% in June, down from May's more than three-year high — but the resumption of the conflict with Iran threaten to push up inflation as energy costs once again spike.

items.title: Supreme Court justices to testify before Congress on increasing security funding in rare appearance
items.summary: Supreme Court Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett are set to appear before Congress

items.title: U.S. to reinstate Hormuz blockade. And, states sue over Paramount-Warner deal
items.summary: The United States plans to reinstate a blockade over the Strait of Hormuz today. And, several states are suing to stop the massive Paramount-Warner Bros. merger.

items.title: Cyclosporiasis Outbreaks Hits More Than Half of the US
items.summary: Cases of a severe stomach bug called Cyclosporiasis are surging across the United States, sickening thousands of Americans and even sending some to the hospital. NBC’s medical contributor Dr. John Torress joins TODAY to break down the symptoms and how you can stay safe.

items.title: Family of a man shot by agents responding to a 911 call seeks answers
items.summary: A troubling pattern of federal agents fatally shooting civilians is developing, with deaths in Maine, Texas and Tennessee. The family of a man shot by agents told NPR they want answers.

items.title: Trump rolls out the White House welcome mat for new Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi
items.summary: President Donald Trump is set to welcome Iraq's new prime minister to the White House

items.title: Oil prices jump as fighting flares in the Middle East

items.title: ICE officer who fatally shot driver in Maine was 'fearing for public safety,' agency says
items.summary: Sen. Angus King says the motorist killed by ICE officers in a Maine shooting was not the target of the warrant the officers were executing

items.title: Busy wildfire season tests US fire bosses as they juggle resources to stay ahead
items.summary: The strategy calls for moving firefighters, engines and aircraft into areas where the risk of wildfire is high in hopes of catching flames before they make a catastrophic run

items.title: New details in Mississippi teen’s death
items.summary: As the investigation into the death of 18-year-old Nolan Wells continues, two friends who were on Horn Island are speaking out. Wells went to the island in a boat on July Fourth and his body was found two days later. NBC News’ Kate Snow has the story.

items.title: Hard drives, car obtained in investigation of shootings of Renee Good, Alex Pretti
items.summary: Minnesota prosecutors obtain hard drives, car in investigation into fatal shootings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti

items.title: US citizen is found guilty of helping export tech to Iran in violation of sanctions
items.summary: A Massachusetts man has been found guilty of conspiring to unlawfully export electronic components to Iran, violating U.S. sanctions

items.title: Who could replace Lindsey Graham? South Carolina's next steps after senator's death
items.summary: Sen. Lindsey Graham's death has sparked a political scramble in South Carolina

items.title: Crash of an aircraft helping to fight a Colorado wildfire leaves the pilot dead
items.summary: Authorities say an aircraft helping to fight a Colorado wildfire has crashed into a reservoir, killing the pilot

items.title: Jordan says it shot down four missiles launched by Iran
items.summary: Jordan says it shot down four missiles launched by Iran

items.title: Man who killed Dartmouth professors at 17 seeks reduced prison sentence
items.summary: A Vermont man who was 17 when he and a friend killed a pair of married Dartmouth College professors 25 years ago wants his life sentence reduced to a minimum of 30 to 40 years

items.title: Senate returns to Washington after Sen. Lindsey Graham's death with uncertain agenda
items.summary: Senate Republicans will return to Washington with an uncertain agenda after the sudden death of prominent Republican Lindsey Graham, a committee chairman and key player who served as a crucial ally with President Donald Trump

items.title: Bahrain sounds its missile alert sirens for a second time as Iran retaliates over US airstrikes
items.summary: Bahrain sounds its missile alert sirens for a second time as Iran retaliates over US airstrikes

items.title: New Year's Eve ball drops in Times Square for America's 250th
items.summary: The New Year's Eve ball in Times Square has dropped for America's 250th anniversary, making this the first time the ball has dropped outside of a New Year's celebration.

items.title: The 8 best UPF sun hats for blocking UV rays
items.summary: UPF hats help protect your face and skin against the sun, similar to topical sunscreen. We spoke with a dermatologist to find the best UPF hats to protect your skin from harmful rays.

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
